description_short: Deliberate integration of trees and shrubs into crop and/or livestock farming systems to generate ecological, economic, and social benefits. Encompasses a diverse family of practices from parkland intercropping to silvopastoralism.
description_technical: Agroforestry encompasses intentional management of woody perennials (trees, shrubs, palms, bamboos) in spatial or temporal combinations with agricultural crops and/or animals on the same land unit. Functional interactions between components are exploited to improve productivity, diversify income streams, sequester carbon, regulate microclimate and hydrology, and enhance biodiversity. Sub-practices include silvoarable (alley cropping, intercropping), silvopastoral (parkland, agrosilvopastoral), and special-purpose systems (windbreaks, riparian buffers, forest farming, home gardens). Suitability is determined by biophysical constraints (climate, soil, slope) and socio-economic context (tenure, market access, labour).

upfront_cost_usd_ha.source_note: Ranges from low-input parkland systems (~$150/ha) to intensively managed silvoarable systems in temperate contexts (~$1200/ha). Marinis et al. (2022) report NPV of CAD$1.186 billion across a landscape-scale intervention. Unger & Lakes (2023) quantify implementation cost savings of assisted natural regeneration vs. tree planting.
